canonical_question: What are the differences between NetSuite TBA (OAuth 1.0) and OAuth 2.0 — deprecation, SOAP limitation?
aliases: NetSuite machine-to-machine authentication OAuth 2.0 client credentials
Which NetSuite auth method should I use — TBA or OAuth 2.0?

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: 2024.2 — TBA removed from SuiteCloud SDK; 2027.1 — no new TBA integrations allowed

constraints: OAuth 2.0 does NOT support SOAP web services — TBA is the only programmatic auth for SOAP
As of 2027.1: no new integrations can use TBA for SOAP, REST, or RESTlets
All SOAP endpoints disabled at 2028.2 — must migrate to REST before then
OAuth 2.0 client credentials (M2M) requires X.509 certificate (RSA 3072 or 4096 bit)
Max 5 active certificates per integration record for M2M flow
M2M setup is not copied between accounts — must re-create in sandbox/Release Preview
OAuth 2.0 access tokens expire (60 min default) — TBA tokens never expire unless revoked

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs NetSuite REST API capabilities and rate limits (not auth-specific)
User needs SuiteScript governance unit limits

often_confused_with.label: Oracle ERP Cloud Authentication — different product, different auth model

inputs_needed.question: What type of integration are you building?
options: user-context (portal, interactive app on behalf of user)

inputs_needed.question: Which NetSuite API surface are you using?
Is this a new integration or migration of an existing one?
options: Migrating existing TBA integration to OAuth 2.0
Maintaining existing SOAP + TBA (need to know sunset dates)
